_doc: Component-token registry (theme schema v2, Layer 4). Each COMMON component is a small param bundle a theme fills so a 'brutalist card' and a 'porcelain card' differ WITHOUT touching block code. The bundle params map to theme tokens the block CSS consumes via var(--token, <default>) — backward-compatible. `status: wired` means every listed token IS consumed by a block (honesty-tested by tests/test_components.py — this is what caught --card-shadow being authored by 24 themes yet dead); `status: pending` names what's still hardcoded + the block it needs. Theme-specific components (retro-windows OS-chrome, 8-bit pixel-stack, sakura seal) live in the extension registry (compose_extension.py), not here.

card._desc: a styled PANEL — its shadow token carries the most character: brutalist hard-offset (neubrutalism 7px/bauhaus 8px), Swiss inset hairline, terminal phosphor glow, or flat (none). Fill/radius/border come from the surface + shape scale.

image-card._desc: gallery / carousel image tile — corner follows the shape scale, but the drop shadow stays block-tuned (image LIFT is a distinct concern from a styled panel; the schema's card vs img-placeholder split).

bullet-marker._desc: the list-item marker glyph (em-dash / slash / chevron / dot / square) — a real per-theme choice (brutalist square vs editorial em-dash vs technical chevron). BLOCKED: no bullet/checklist block exists yet; add one, then tokenise --bullet-marker.

timeline-dot._desc: the node marker on a timeline spine — ring width + fill. Currently .tlnode hardcodes 28px / 5px accent ring / #0a0b0a fill (the Vox cinematic variant); tokenise ring-weight + fill so a flat theme gets a flat dot.

pill._desc: tag / pill chip — fill/border/radius/padding. Several blocks hardcode a pill (dgnode.pill 999px, kicker chips); unify to a --pill-* bundle.

bar._desc: data bar track + fill (bar chart / progress). --ch-bar / .lt-tab hardcode radius + accent fill; tokenise track + fill + cap-radius.

counter._desc: page number / ordinal counter (the 01/02 markers + background-ordinal decoration). Type comes from the type-role system; a --counter-* bundle would carry its own weight/opacity.
